tracker.ticketKeyNote: ICR-N is a DERIVED display key: N == the Trello card's idShort. There is no native 'ICR-N' field. To fetch a card from key ICR-N: set_active_board(boardId), then resolve the card whose idShort === N (scan lists via get_cards_by_list_id, or get_card once the id is known). Branches/PRs/commits use the ICR-N string; all Trello WRITE calls use the resolved card id.
tracker.listResolutionNote: Trello cards move between LISTS by listId (move_card takes the destination listId), not by status name. Always use the listId values above. The name fields are display-only — 'Dsicovery' is misspelled on the real board; do NOT 'correct' it. If a listId 404s at runtime, re-fetch via get_lists(boardId), match by name, surface the drift — never invent an id.

workflow.name: In Review
workflow.setBy: /work step 14 via pr-author (In Progress -> In Review, at PR-ready)

workflow.name: Done
workflow.setBy: HUMAN ONLY — set when the human merges the PR and closes the card. NEVER set by any agent or command.

automatedTransitions.gate: after PR flipped to ready + PR-link comment posted

forbiddenTransitions.reason: Human-only. /work and all agents MUST NOT move any card to Done. Done == merged and closed by a human.

Integration.note: feat when it adds user-facing capability; chore when it's wiring/config only

NFR.note: default chore; use refactor/perf when the work is purely that

tracker.needsRefinementNote: The 4 board labels (Feature/Bug/Integration/NFR) are reserved for commit-type. Refinement state is structural: an intake card carries a 'Refinement' checklist with an unchecked 'needs-refinement' item; refine clears it. A To Do card with no open needs-refinement item is the /work-ready signal.
tracker.humanGateNote: Every tracker WRITE (move_card, add_comment, update_card_details) happens at or after a human gate. /work owns exactly two automated moves (todo->inProgress, inProgress->inReview) and posts the PR-link comment at PR-ready. It NEVER moves a card to Done.

qaDepth.sourceNote: Trello has no native 'QA Depth' field. Resolve in priority order: (1) a custom field 'QA Depth' if get_board_custom_fields exposes one; (2) a label or a 'QA: <depth>' token in the card description/checklist; (3) fall back to qaDepth.default. Reject values not in qaDepth.allowed.

qaLoop._note: Drives /qa + qa-acceptance. Reads the top-level tracker block for board/list ids. SHIPS IN PHASE 1 REPORT-ONLY: enabledModes=[report]; seed/fix/auto recognized but gated. QA target is Vercel PREVIEW DEPLOYMENTS (per-PR) — there is NO separate staging env. autoMerge disabled (kill switch).

preview.requirePreviewEnvironmentNote: The hostname allowlist is NECESSARY BUT NOT SUFFICIENT: the production deployment ALSO has a *.vercel.app alias (e.g. idc-redentor-website.vercel.app), which the regex would otherwise accept. Before ANY browser/API/seed action the resolver MUST confirm the resolved deployment is a PREVIEW, not Production — check mcp__claude_ai_Vercel__get_deployment.target !== 'production' (or the GitHub deployment environment === 'Preview'). Reject any deployment whose target/environment is Production even when its host ends in .vercel.app. Prefer the per-PR branch preview whose host contains '-git-<branch>-' or a unique deployment hash.
preview.baseUrlHostAllowNote: Default-deny allowlist for the QA base URL host. /qa runs ONLY against a Vercel PREVIEW deployment for the PR. Three gates, all required: (1) host matches baseUrlHostAllow (*.vercel.app); (2) host is NOT in productionHostDeny (custom domains AND the production *.vercel.app aliases); (3) the deployment's target/environment is Preview (requirePreviewEnvironment). Preview URL is discovered per-PR via mcp__claude_ai_Vercel__list_deployments / get_deployment (target=preview), or the GitHub deployment status on the PR.
preview.dbNote: Mongo backs ONLY the blog 'likes' + saved 'contact' messages, in a DB literally named 'website'. The allowlist excludes 'website' so QA never reads/writes production data. Phase 1: no Mongo writes at all.

transitions._note: Trello list moves by listId. Phase 1 report-only: /qa may move To Do/In Progress -> In Review, never Done. When fix/auto land later, onRemediationReady moves inProgress->inReview via pr-author.

autoMerge.killSwitchNote: Phase 1 kill switch. Even when modes fix/auto are enabled later, autoMerge stays false until explicitly flipped — a human merges every PR.

commandsNote: typecheck maps to ICR's actual 'type-check' script (hyphen). 'test' = vitest run (single-pass; package.json 'test' is 'vitest run', never watch). 'e2e' = playwright test (config present, no specs in Phase 1). lint = eslint . If a script is absent at runtime, verifier/qa-runner reports it rather than inventing one.

playwrightProjectMap._note: ICR uses src/app/[locale]/<segment> (NO route groups) plus src/app/api/<route>. Phase 1 has NO e2e suite — qa-runner authors specs per-ticket on heavy depth. This map tells qa-runner which e2e project/tag to target for a given changed path.

graphify.enabledNote: auto = detect at runtime by checking ${MAIN_REPO_ROOT}/graphify-out/graph.json. false to skip; true to require.
graphify.staleWarnNote: Code freshness is handled continuously by the git post-commit hook (AST re-extract, no LLM). staleWarnSeconds targets SEMANTIC drift — doc/content/markdown changes that only a full `graphify update` re-extracts. Agents warn past this threshold and Grep to confirm negatives.
graphify.updateCommandNote: The CLI takes a SUBCOMMAND + path: `graphify update <root>`. The `graphify --update` flag form is the /graphify SKILL interface, NOT the raw CLI — passing it makes the CLI parse the path as an unknown command and silently error. Always invoke as `<updateCommand> "<root>"`.
graphify.verbsNote: Match code symbols by their node label INCLUDING parens, e.g. getPage(). query = BFS context for a question (default read path). explain "X()" = single-node onboarding: the symbol + neighbours WITH direction (`<-- caller [imports|calls]` is its dependents) — the RELIABLE impact lookup on this graph. path "A()" "B()" = shortest dependency path (data-flow traces). saveResult = persist a graph-answered Q&A to graphify-out/memory/ so repeat questions get cheaper. affected "X()" = reverse-traversal blast-radius, but only works on a DIRECTED graph; this repo's graph is currently undirected (directed:false), so prefer explain. To enable affected, rebuild once with `/graphify --directed` (graphify update preserves directedness).

policy.whoRefreshes: Continuous: the git post-commit hook (`graphify hook install`) re-extracts changed code on every commit (AST, no LLM cost). Per-session: the first /work that gets the lock additionally runs `graphify update` to catch doc/content (semantic) drift; concurrent sessions read the existing graph.
policy.whoQueries: explorer always (query/explain/path when available); implementer for caller/dep + `explain` impact lookups before edits/deletes (confirm negatives with Grep); security-reviewer for `explain` blast-radius on changed shared symbols in the diff; product-manager for reuse lookups; verifier/qa-runner/pr-author never. (`affected` is the dedicated blast-radius verb but needs a directed graph — see verbsNote.)
policy.onFailureFallback: Proceed with existing graph (or Grep/Read if no graph). Agents include staleness/error notes in their reports and Grep to confirm any 'not found' negative when the graph may be stale.
policy.memoryLoop: After answering a codebase question from the graph, the querying agent runs `graphify save-result --question ... --answer ... --nodes ...` so the next `graphify update` folds the Q&A back into the graph.
